Religion can foster social cohesion through shared values and communities.

Religion has historically contributed to community cohesion and moral frameworks that have shaped many societies. Its role remains significant for many, though not all, depend on it for those values and social structures.

Religion has historically provided social cohesion and moral frameworks, but its necessity depends on alternative institutions like law and civil society. Modern pluralistic societies can thrive with secular ethics, yet religious communities often remain vital sources of charity and community support.
Religion can be important for social cohesion and moral guidance in many communities. However, a good society can also exist through secular values and laws that respect all beliefs equally.
Religion can contribute to social cohesion through shared values and community support. It plays a role in a good society.
